doc_title: Extreme Compression of Large Language Models via Additive Quantization

items.fact: Additive Quantization (AQ) (Babenko & Lempitsky, 2014; Martinez et al., 2016) is referenced
items.evidence_span: we extend Additive Quantization (AQ) (Babenko \& Lempitsky, 2014; Martinez et al., 2016), a popular MCQ algorithm, to the task of compressing LLM weights
Residual Vector Quantization (Chen et al., 2010), quantizes original vectors, and then iteratively quantizes the approximation residuals from the previous iteration.

Non-orthogonal quantizations. Follow-up work (Chen et al., 2010; Babenko \& Lempitsky, 2014; Martinez et al., 2016; Zhang et al., 2014; Ozan et al., 2016; Martinez et al., 2018) generalized the idea of Product Quantization by approximating each vector by a sum of $M$ codewords instead of concatenation.

Non-orthogonal quantizations. Follow-up work (Chen et al., 2010; Babenko \& Lempitsky, 2014; Martinez et al., 2016; Zhang et al., 2014; Ozan et al., 2016; Martinez et al., 2018) generalized the idea of Product Quantization by approximating each vector by a sum of $M$ codewords instead of concatenation.
